page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv8-based AWS Graviton2 (Neoverse-N1) processor architecture running at 2.5 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The server is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 72845.21. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with geekbench single-core scoring 943.0 (weak) and stress-ng single-core scoring 1519.49 (average). Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show weak to poor performance, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches an average tier of 63.81 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth remains average across both small cached blocks (2838.51 GB/sec) and large uncached blocks (148.11 GB/sec read, 71.03 GB/sec write).

Given the high core density and dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that benefit from local NVMe SSD storage. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations (scoring 20461.19 on PassMark database operations), static web serving (39.7 GB/sec throughput), and software compilation. It is less suitable for single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM prompt processing, where it exhibits poor performance.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 (Neoverse-N1) architecture running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 72845.21. However, single-core performance is a key tradeoff, with geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression benchmarks falling into the weak and poor categories. The server provides average performance for memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database applications that leverage local high-speed NVMe storage, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 server with 48 dedicated cores, 96 GB RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring dedicated physical cores and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU performance with average memory bandwidth
Tradeoff of weaker single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
Optimized for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and software compilation
